courseleaf.title: Forensic Entomology, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The Department of Entomology & Nematology has six minor programs open to students in other disciplines who are seeking to complement their academic studies with focused training.

Minor Advisor

Lead Faculty Advisors

Shahid Saddique, Ph.D., Rachel L. Vannette, Ph.D., Vice Chair. 

Academic Advisor

Elvira Galvan Hack; 160 Hutchison Hall; 530-754-7277; Entomology & Nematology Advising.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
ENT 100General Entomology4
ENT 100LGeneral Entomology Laboratory 2
ENT 102Insect Physiology 4
ENT 104Behavioral Ecology of Insects 3
ENT 158Forensic Entomology3
Choose one:4
ENT 105
Insect Ecology
EVE 101
Introduction to Ecology
ESP 100
General Ecology
Total Units20
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Students interested in forensic science and/or legal careers benefit from the Forensic Entomology minor. The taxonomy, development, behavior, and distribution of arthropods can provide insight and evidence relevant to criminal activity and other matters of litigation.
